mission.title: Install and Configure Kubefleet on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeFleet is an open-source Kubernetes multi-cluster application management solution that simplifies governance and workload management across multiple clusters. Installing KubeFleet allows you to efficiently manage applications in a hybrid or multi-cloud environment.

steps.title: Add the KubeFleet Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official KubeFleet Helm repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add kubefleet https://charts.kubefleet.dev
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the KubeFleet hub agent
steps.description:
Install the KubeFleet hub agent using Helm, specifying the namespace and version:
```bash
helm install hub-agent kubefleet/hub-agent --namespace fleet-system --create-namespace --version 0.1.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the hub agent pod is running correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace fleet-system
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for the hub agent
steps.description:
Update the resource limits for the hub agent by editing the Helm release:
```bash
helm upgrade hub-agent kubefleet/hub-agent --namespace fleet-system --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=1Gi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for KubeFleet
steps.description:
Create a ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding for the KubeFleet hub agent:
```bash
kubectl create clusterrole hub-agent-role --verb=get,list,watch,create,update,patch,delete --resource=pods,services,deployments
kubectl create clusterrolebinding hub-agent-role-binding --clusterrole=hub-agent-role --serviceaccount=fleet-system:default
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeFleet will show the hub agent pod running in the 'fleet-system' namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and RBAC for the hub agent.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubefleet https://charts.kubefleet.dev
helm repo update
helm upgrade hub-agent kubefleet/hub-agent --namespace fleet-system --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=1Gi

kubectl create clusterrole hub-agent-role --verb=get,list,watch,create,update,patch,delete --resource=pods,services,deployments
kubectl create clusterrolebinding hub-agent-role-binding --clusterrole=hub-agent-role --serviceaccount=fleet-system:default

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the KubeFleet hub agent Helm release to remove the deployment from your cluster. Run the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall hub-agent --namespace fleet-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent resources that were created by KubeFleet. Use the following commands to remove them:
```bash
kubectl delete crd <crd-name>
# Replace <crd-name> with the actual CRD names associated with KubeFleet
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for KubeFleet and verify that it has been removed. Execute the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace fleet-system
kubectl get namespaces
# Ensure 'fleet-system' is no longer listed
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the KubeFleet hub agent. You can do this by exporting the current configuration:
```bash
helm get values hub-agent --namespace fleet-system > hub-agent-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the KubeFleet hub agent to the latest version. Execute the following commands:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade hub-agent kubefleet/hub-agent --namespace fleet-system --version 0.2.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After upgrading, verify that the hub agent pod is running with the new version. Use the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace fleet-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the hub agent pods are stuck in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors. Use the following command to view logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace fleet-system
# Replace <pod-name> with the name of the hub agent pod
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails due to a version conflict, ensure that the specified version is available in the repository. Check available versions with:
```bash
helm search repo kubefleet/hub-agent --versions
```

troubleshooting.title: RBAC permissions issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter RBAC permission issues, verify that the ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding are correctly set up. Check the bindings with:
```bash
kubectl get clusterrolebinding hub-agent-role-binding
```

troubleshooting.title: Namespace not found error
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive a 'namespace not found' error, ensure that the 'fleet-system' namespace exists. You can check existing namespaces with:
```bash
kubectl get namespaces
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
